title: acourtia longifolia
intro: Acourtia longifolia, also known as yerba de pasmo, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the daisy and sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Acourtia longifolia include its ability to ease coughs, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. Herbal preparations of Acourtia longifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate respiratory discomfort and promote overall well-being.
